title: archidendron clypearia subsp. clypearia
intro: Archidendron clypearia subsp. clypearia, also known as the Koompassia tree or locally as 'Tembeling' in Malaysia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, where its leaves and roots are used to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties. The leaves of the Archidendron clypearia subsp. clypearia are known to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against cell damage. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Archidendron clypearia subsp. clypearia include decoctions made from the leaves and roots, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
